page: Microsoft Azure L80s_v2 is an LSv2-series virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor. It features 80 vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For high-capacity local data requirements, the instance includes 20019 GB of bundled local storage, managed under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a clear performance profile characterized by strong multi-threaded capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. The single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% to 25% according to stress-ng and Geekbench tests, reflecting the older AMD EPYC microarchitecture. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption, rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform in the middle 50% tier, indicating stable throughput for parallelized workloads despite the lower single-thread speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L80s_v2 offers high resource density, combining massive local storage capacity with a substantial memory footprint. This makes it a viable option for large-scale data processing where local storage speed and capacity are prioritized over raw single-threaded CPU performance. The hardware configuration is best suited for large databases, big data analytics, multi-threaded data compression pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that can leverage parallel processing across its 80 vCPUs.
description: Microsoft Azure L80s_v2 is an LSv2-series virtual machine designed for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 80 vCPUs and 40 physical cores running at 3.2 GHz, this x86_64 instance features 640.0 GB of RAM and 20019 GB of bundled local storage. While single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, ranking in the bottom tier of benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong multi-threaded performance, particularly in encryption and decompression tasks. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform consistently in the average tier. This resource profile offers high storage and memory density, making the L80s_v2 highly suitable for large-scale databases, parallelized data processing, and big data analytics workloads that require substantial local storage capacity without needing top-tier single-threaded compute speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L80s_v2 is an LSv2 virtual machine featuring 80 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and 20019 GB of local storage, optimized for large-scale databases and parallel data processing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L80s_v2 features 80 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and 20019 GB local storage, optimized for storage-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory virtual machine featuring eighty vCPUs and over twenty terabytes of local storage for parallelized database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 80 vCPUs and 40 physical cores.
Equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory and 20019 GB of bundled local storage.
Strong multi-threaded performance in encryption and decompression benchmarks.
Lower single-core CPU performance relative to modern hardware generations.
Well-suited for large-scale databases, big data analytics, and parallel processing.
